error.signature: MISCONF Redis is configured to save RDB snapshots, but it can't persist to disk

verdict.summary: Redis cannot save to disk. Disk full, permission error, or insufficient memory for BGSAVE fork.

dead_ends.action: Disable persistence entirely with CONFIG SET save ''
dead_ends.why_fails: Data will be lost on restart. Only appropriate for pure cache use cases.
Allows writes to continue but data is not being persisted; silent data loss risk

workarounds.how: df -h /var/lib/redis  # check disk; free -m  # check memory for fork

workarounds.action: Set vm.overcommit_memory=1 for Linux fork behavior
workarounds.how: echo 1 > /proc/sys/vm/overcommit_memory  # allows Redis BGSAVE fork to succeed

workarounds.action: Use AOF persistence as alternative if RDB fork fails
workarounds.how: CONFIG SET appendonly yes  # AOF does not require fork for every write
